---
title: "Series"
subtitle: "Three arcs, one goal: turn a business question into a defensible number."
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

Most posts here belong to one of three ongoing arcs. Each arc has its
own reading order, so you can start from the beginning or jump to what
you need.

Pick an arc from the **Series** menu at the top of the page.
If you're new here, start with **Causal Inference** — it's the
foundation the other two build on.

::: {.grid .series-grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.series-card .card-causal}
### 🟢 Causal Inference

Identification strategies, potential outcomes, DAGs, and the methods
that let you defend a number in front of a skeptical stakeholder.

**Covers:** A/B testing, DiD, RDD, IV, synthetic control, matching,
G-computation, DML, causal forests, uplift modelling.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.series-card .card-pricing}
### 🟠 Pricing & Demand

Price elasticity, two-sided market pricing, dynamic pricing, MMM, and
the causal experiments that make pricing decisions defensible.

**Covers:** elasticity estimation, personalized pricing, MMM, geo
experiments, revenue optimization, marketplace dynamics.
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.series-card .card-forecasting}
### 🔵 Forecasting

Probabilistic forecasting, model evaluation, and where forecasting
stops being causal inference. From ETS to foundation models, with
honesty about what each can and cannot do.

**Covers:** ETS, ARIMA, hierarchical reconciliation, probabilistic
forecasting, conformal prediction, foundation models.
:::
:::

:::

---

## How the arcs connect

The three arcs are separate but they answer complementary questions.

- **Causal inference** asks *what would have happened otherwise?*
- **Pricing** asks *what should we do?*, and reuses causal methods to
  measure the effect of price and marketing decisions.
- **Forecasting** asks *what is likely to happen?*, and feeds both
  pricing decisions and experimentation planning.

---

*New posts publish weekly. If you want them in your inbox, subscribe
via the index.xml or drop me a message on
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/joao-vieira-iseg/).*
